popularStory.description: John Mor MacDonald was the second son of Good John of Islay, Lord of the Isles, 6th chief of Clan Donald, through John of Islays second marriage to Princess Margaret Stewart, daughter of King Robert I …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg於大約1352年出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾時，他的父親John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles33歲，母親Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland17歲。他於1399年與Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim在愛爾蘭庫迪 安特里姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1427年登記服兵役。他去世於1427年在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特易斯萊享年76歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg于大约1352年出生在苏格兰阿吉爾时，他的父亲John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles33岁，母亲Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland17岁。他于1399年与Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim在爱尔兰庫迪 安特里姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1427年登记服兵役。他去世于1427年在英国苏格兰阿吉爾 安德 布特易斯萊享年76岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: MacDonald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McDonald
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Domhnaill ‘son of Domhnall’, a name derived from the Celtic elements domno- ‘world’ + val- ‘might, rule’. Donald is an Anglicized form (via Latin) of this personal name used in Scotland, though the surname is also widespread in Ireland. The name is equivalent to Irish McDonnell and McConnell , and to Manx Cannell .

History: This is the name of the largest and most disparate of the Scottish clans (Clan Donald), associated in particular with the Hebrides and claiming descent from Domhnall mac Raghnaill mac Somhairle, who lived in the late 12th century. From that time until 1493 the head of the clan was known as Lord (or King) of the Isles. The reigns of the Lords of the Isles were always stormy, often in conflict with the kings of Scotland, and peppered with disasters. After a series of defeats in the 1480s, Eoin Mac Dhomhnaill a Ìle (John Macdonald of Islay, Earl of Ross and Lord of the Isles) forfeited his lands, his power, and his title as Lord of the Isles in 1493 to King James IV of Scotland. By then, a branch of the family had settled in the Antrim Glens in Ireland and members moved between the southern Hebrides and Ireland throughout the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Mor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from mór ‘large, great’. This was the commonest of all girls’ names in late medieval Ireland, and has continued in use in both Ireland and Scotland to the present day.

nameMeanings.name: 'the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Tanister'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Bannister  .
History: The naturalist John Banister (1650–92) was born in Gloucestershire, England, and came to VA in 1678.

variants.name: Bannister
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from Old French banaste, banastre ‘covering for a cart or wagon; basket’, i.e. a large wicker container. In the 12th century a Norman family of this name had estates in Orne, Normandy, and in England. Ricardus Banastre appears in charters relating to the Earls of Chester c. 1120–29. With what sense the Norman surname was acquired is unknown. It can hardly have been occupational, contrary to Reaney's view that it denoted a basket maker. It is possible that many or even all of the later bearers of the surname were descended from this knightly family. However, several men with this surname in the 14th-century Poll Tax Returns are described as servants or agricultural laborers, while Ricardus Banastr', recorded in 1381 was a butcher. It is conceivable that these men took their name from Middle English banastre, a borrowing of the French word, and that it referred to a basket or hamper they used in their work. Alternatively, they may have belonged to branches of the knightly family that had fallen in the social scale. The term denoting a stair rail is unconnected with this name; it was not used before the 17th century.

en.lifeSummary: When John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg was born about 1352, in Argyll, Scotland, his father, John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles, was 33 and his mother, Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland, was 17. He married Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim in 1399, in County Antrim, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1427. He died in 1427, in Islay, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg est né environ 1352, à Argyll, Écosse, son père, John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim en 1399, à County Antrim, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1427. Il est décédé en 1427, à Islay, Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.Ard-du, Islay, Argyll, Scotland: Islay, Argyll and Bute, Schottland, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg um 1352 in Argyll, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles 33 und seine Mutter Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland 17. Er hat 1399 in County Antrim, Irland Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1427 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1427 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Islay, Argyll and Bute, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

Lui sposato Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim nel 1399, nel County Antrim, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1427. ja.lifeSummary: John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyvegが約1352年にスコットランドアルグイルで生まれた時，父John Carrach MacDonald of the Islesは33歳，母Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotlandは17歳であった。1399年にアイルランドコウントイ アントリムでMargaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrimと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1427年に兵役登録をした。彼は1427年にイギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトイスレイで76歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.Ard-du, Islay, Argyll, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 이슬레이
ko.lifeSummary: John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg(이)가 대략 1352년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1399년 아일랜드 카운티 앤트림에서 Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1427년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1427년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 이슬레이에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.Ard-du, Islay, Argyll, Scotland: Islay, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg nasceu aproximadamente 1352, em Argyll, Scotland, seu pai, John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim em 1399, em County Antrim, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1427. Ele faleceu em 1427, em Islay, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 76 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.Ard-du, Islay, Argyll, Scotland: ислеи, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Mor 'the Tanister' MacDonald of Dunnyveg родился около 1352 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim в 1399 г. в местности коунти антрим, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. В 1427 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1427 г. в местности ислеи, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

Se casó con Margaret Bisset, heiress of the Glens of Antrim en 1399, en County Antrim, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1427. Murió en 1427, en Islay, Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
